meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Client Workflow to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank screen. Learn how a professional client workflow helps students build real-world SaaS apps using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The secret to beating 'blank canvas' syndrome isn't imagination—it's process. By adopting a professional client workflow, you transform abstract requests into structured requirements. Use Antigravity to define your architecture and Claude Code to execute the build, turning your portfolio into a magnet for future employers and high-ticket clients.
content.intro:
The blinking cursor on an empty editor is the silent killer of student ambitions. You have the technical skills, but you lack the map to turn code into a functional product. It’s a paralyzing state where 'no project ideas' feels like a lack of talent, when in reality, it is simply a lack of a framework.

Today, we break that cycle. We are stepping out of the tutorial hell loop and into the shoes of a lead architect. By applying a rigorous client workflow—even for your own projects—you stop guessing what to build and start engineering solutions that actually matter.

steps.title: Define the Value Proposition
steps.description: Identify a specific pain point a user faces. We treat your project as a client job, defining the 'Why' before the 'How'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Manager. Generate 3 SaaS project ideas that solve a specific problem for small business owners, including a core feature list for each.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project requirements document for a CRM tool for local coffee shops.'

steps.title: Architect the Database Schema
steps.description: Structure your data before writing a single line of application code to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a relational database schema (SQL) for a multi-tenant application that manages client appointments and billing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create prisma schema based on the generated requirements.'

steps.title: Build the MVP Scaffold
steps.description: Use AI tools to bootstrap your project structure, ensuring best practices for folder organization are met.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project structure using TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI, optimized for high performance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize a new Next.js project with the specified stack and folder structure.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Feature
steps.description: Focus on the 'Money Maker' feature—the one thing that solves the primary problem for your target user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, modular component for an appointment scheduling form that validates user input and connects to the backend API.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate the appointment booking component and integrate it with the database API route.'

steps.title: Testing and Refinement
steps.description: Simulate a client UAT (User Acceptance Testing) phase to identify bugs and UX friction points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write unit tests for the appointment booking logic to ensure edge cases like double-bookings are handled correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Write Jest tests for the booking service and fix any identified logic errors.'

pro_tips: Always build for a 'fictional client'—it forces you to prioritize documentation and maintainability over hacks.
Use Claude Code to generate your README.md immediately; documentation is what separates a student project from a professional product.
Limit your MVP scope to exactly one primary user workflow; avoid feature creep at all costs.
Commit your code after every single functional milestone; your Git history is your most valuable proof of work for recruiters.
Integrate error logging early; professional apps are defined by how they handle failure, not just success.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to Vercel, share the live link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the 'client' problem you solved, and start your next project.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build anything from a simple tool to a complex SaaS. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the solution. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
